news.headline: International Flavors & Fragrances (NYSE:IFF) stock down in premarket. Q4 earnings: 97 cents/share, sales $2.771B (+3% YoY).
news.summary: Shares of International Flavors & Fragrances, Inc. (NYSE:IFF) are down in premarket trading following the release of their fourth-quarter financial results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 97 cents, surpassing the expected 81 cents. Sales for the quarter totaled $2.771 billion, showing a 3% increase compared to the previous year and exceeding the analyst consensus of $2.677 billion. Currency-neutral sales saw a 6% growth year over year, driven by strong performance across all divisions. The Nourish Segment alone generated $1.41 billion in sales, with a 4% increase on a currency-neutral basis. This information was reported yesterday.

news.headline: Report on Agriculture Customer Segmentation Market Analysis by ResearchAndMarkets - focuses on farm size, farmer age group, crop type, and farm ownership.
news.summary: Dublin - A report titled "Agriculture Customer Segmentation Market" has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com, offering a global and regional analysis focusing on farm size, farmer age group, crop type, and farm ownership. This market involves categorizing farmers and stakeholders into groups based on shared characteristics to customize products, services, and marketing strategies. The analysis period for this report is 2024. For more information and insights on this market, visit ResearchAndMarkets.com.

news.headline: Ex-SEC member predicts crypto probes to halt as agency halts Ripple lawsuit appeal soon. #cryptocurrency #regulation
news.summary: John Reed Stark, a former attorney at the SEC, anticipates a forthcoming delay in the agency's appeal against Ripple.

news.headline: MANTRA Group secures first VARA DeFi license in Dubai, allowing global expansion and innovation in financial products.
news.summary: In Dubai, UAE, on February 19, 2025, MANTRA Finance FZE (MANTRA), a prominent decentralized finance (DeFi) platform under the MANTRA Group, revealed acquiring a Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license from Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority.

news.headline: Amicus Therapeutics announces 2024 financial results, total revenue $528.3M, 33% YoY growth. Anticipates positive GAAP net income by H2 2025.
news.summary: Amicus Therapeutics reported total revenue of $528.3 million in 2024, a 33% increase year-over-year. They anticipate achieving positive GAAP net income during the second half of 2025. The company's total revenue growth guidance for 2025 is projected to be between 17-24%. Galafold net product sales in 2024 were $458.1 million, up 18% year-over-year. Pombiliti + Opfolda net product sales were $70.2 million in 2024, reflecting a significant increase. The company aims to exceed $1 billion in total sales by 2028. Their non-GAAP operating expenses for 2024 were $347.8 million. Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $249.9 million at the end of 2024. Amicus Therapeutics will host a conference call and webcast to discuss their financial results for 2024. The company's focus is on developing and delivering novel medicines for rare diseases. They also provided financial guidance for 2025, including revenue growth projections and operating expenses.

news.headline: OGE Energy Corp. reported earnings of $2.19 per diluted share in 2024 (compared to $2.07 in 2023) with net income of $441.5 million. OG&E had earnings of $2.33 per diluted share in 2024. The Company forecasts a consolidated earnings per share growth rate of 5-7% for 2025.
news.summary: OGE Energy Corp. reported earnings of $2.19 per diluted share in 2024, increased from $2.07 in 2023. OG&E, a segment of OGE, contributed $2.33 per diluted share, up from $2.12 in 2023, while other operations reflected a loss of $0.14 per diluted share, compared to 2023's loss of $0.05. The net income for OG&E in 2024 was $469.9 million, up from $426.4 million in 2023. Overall, OGE Energy's net income for 2024 was $441.5 million or $2.19 per diluted share, compared to $416.8 million or $2.07 in 2023. For the fourth quarter of 2024, OGE Energy reported net income of $101.9 million, an increase from $48.2 million in the same period of 2023. The company expects consolidated earnings per share growth rate between 5-7% annually from the midpoint of the 2025 guidance range of $2.27 per average diluted share. OG&E, with approximately 907,000 customers, declared a second-quarter dividend of $0.42125, payable on April 25, 2025. The 2025 financial results guidance and further details are available in OGE Energy Corp.'s Form 10-K. Some matters discussed in the article contain "forward-looking statements" that are subject to risks and uncertainties. Factors affecting OGE's results include economic conditions, operational and regulatory factors, environmental laws, and more. The Company will be hosting a conference call on February 19, 2025.

news.headline: Stepan reported Q4 net income of $3.4M, up from a $1.2M loss. Adjusted EBITDA was $35.8M, up 39% YoY. YTD net income was $50.4M, up 25%. Global sales volume increased 1%. Polymer net sales decreased by 12%, while Specialty Products saw a 10% increase. Company delivered $13M in cost savings in Q4. CEO mentioned strategic investments and steps to return to growth. Positivity for future growth in the Surfactant business. EBITDA expected to improve in all reporting segments in 2025. Surfactant operating income grew by 9% in the quarter. Polymers saw a 73% decrease in operating income due to demand shortfall. Specialty Products saw a 102% increase in operating income.公司经历了2024年的整体财务表现并不理想, 但取得了一些进展, 政策，以及为未来增长提供的机会。
news.summary: Stepan Company reported a net income of $3.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 as compared to a $1.2 million net loss in the previous year. Adjusted net income was $2.8 million, down 63% from the prior year. EBITDA was $35.8 million, up 39%, and Adjusted EBITDA was $35.0 million, down 7% year-over-year. Global sales volume decreased by 1% year-over-year. Cash from Operations was $68.3 million, and free cash flow for the quarter was $32.1 million. For the full year, reported net income was $50.4 million, up 25% from the previous year. Adjusted net income was flat, and EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA increased by 13% and 4% respectively year-over-year. The company also delivered cost out savings of $13.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. CEO Luis E. Rojo expressed disappointment in the financial performance in 2024 but emphasized strategic investments for future growth. Surfactants and Specialty Products showed strong growth, while Polymers experienced demand weakness. Stepan is optimistic about 2025, focusing on growth strategies, cost reductions, and delivery of improved financial performance.

news.headline: Etsy reports highest-ever revenue in Q4 2024, with $3.3 billion in marketplace GMS, 89.6M active buyers, and $852.2M consolidated revenue. Record net income of $129.9M, 29.4% non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA margin. Depop contributed to 31.6% full year GMS growth. Q1 2025 GMS expected to decline similar to Q4 2024. Etsy continues to focus on buyer engagement and personalization.
news.summary: Etsy, Inc. reported record quarterly revenue in Q4 2024, with consolidated revenue reaching $852.2 million, 1.2% higher than Q4 2023. Despite facing challenges like a decline in Gross Merchandise Sales (GMS) by 6.8%, Etsy's profitability improved significantly. The company reactivated a record 9.8 million lapsed buyers and acquired 6.9 million new buyers. Consolidated net income rose by 56% to $129.9 million, with a 15.2% net income margin. Active buyers decreased by 2.6% to 89.6 million. Etsy also acquired Depop, which contributed to a full-year GMS growth of 31.6%. The company ended the year with $1.2 billion in cash, repurchasing around $260 million of its common stock. Etsy's focus for 2025 is to increase buyer engagement, sales, and accelerate GMS growth. Depop saw accelerated growth after seller fee changes, while Reverb experienced robust performance during the holiday season. The company anticipates a decline in consolidated GMS for Q1 2025 but aims to improve buyer engagement and drive sales.

news.headline: Garmin reports impressive full-year revenue of $6.30 billion, a 20% increase compared to the previous year. Q4 revenue reached $1.82 billion, with a 23% increase from the prior year quarter. Operating income for Q4 was $516 million, a 52% increase, and pro forma EPS grew by 40%. Garmin expects full-year 2025 revenue to reach $6.80 billion. Dividend to increase by 20%.
news.summary: Garmin Ltd. announced its fourth quarter and fiscal year results for 2024, reporting record revenue and profit. For the fourth quarter, consolidated revenue was $1.82 billion, a 23% increase from the previous year. The company achieved an operating income of $516 million, a 52% increase from the prior year. Additionally, for the fiscal year 2024, Garmin reported consolidated revenue of $6.30 billion, a 20% increase year-over-year, with record operating income of $1.59 billion. The company also proposed a 20% dividend increase, with a cash dividend of $3.60 per share to be paid in four installments. Garmin projects full-year 2025 revenue to be approximately $6.80 billion, with a pro forma EPS of $7.80. The company's strong performance was attributed to growth in all segments, with notable product launches such as the Approach R50 and Descent X50i contributing to revenue growth.

news.headline: Mastech Digital reports Q4 revenue growth of 10.2%, with $50.7M total revenues and $10.3M from Data and Analytics Services segment. Good financial performance with increased gross margins. Share repurchase program extended until 2026. Cash balances of $27.7M. Non-GAAP net income for Q4 was $2.8M. Total revenue for 2024 was $198.9M. Non-GAAP measures used to supplement financial results. Business segments ended well in 2024. Bookings were lower than expected due to market uncertainties. Company focusing on improving financial performance and transparency.
news.summary: Mastech Digital, Inc. reported financial results for Q4 2024 with total revenue increasing by 10.2% to $50.7 million compared to Q4 2023, with Data and Analytics Services segment revenue growing by 26.2% to $10.3 million. The IT Staffing Services segment revenue reached $40.5 million, a 6.8% YoY increase. GAAP diluted earnings per share were $0.02, a notable improvement from a loss of ($0.46) in Q4 2023, while non-GAAP diluted earnings per share rose to $0.23 from $0.11 in Q4 2023. For FY 2024, total revenue was $198.9 million, with GAAP net income at $3.4 million and non-GAAP net income at $8.6 million. The company had $27.7 million in cash balances on Dec 31, 2024, no bank debt, and borrowing availability of $22.6 million. The Board of Directors extended the share repurchase program until Feb 8, 2026. Non-GAAP measures were used in financial reporting, with net income and diluted earnings per share excluding expenses like amortization of intangible assets, stock-based compensation, severance charges, impairment of goodwill, and settlement reserves. Forward-looking statements were made regarding revenue generation, earnings, and cash flow, discussing risks associated with market demand, competition, economic conditions, and pandemics. The company's assets as of Dec 31, 2024, totaled $111.484 million.

news.headline: Lazard hires Chris Miller as Managing Director in Power, Energy & Infrastructure Advisory, focusing on Oil & Gas.
news.summary: Lazard, Inc. revealed that Chris Miller has been appointed as a Managing Director in the Power, Energy & Infrastructure Advisory division, stationed in Houston. Miller will focus on advising clients in the Oil & Gas sector. His recruitment underscores Lazard's effective global hiring initiatives amid the firm's sustained growth. This move showcases Lazard's commitment to strengthening its team to enhance client services in the Energy industry. Chris Miller's appointment highlights Lazard's strategic approach to expanding its expertise in the sector.

news.headline: Stablepharma receives €2.5m grant for fridge-free vaccine tech, named top European innovator.
news.summary: A groundbreaking Fridge-free Vaccine Technology developed by Stablepharma has been awarded a 2.5 million euro grant from the European Innovation Council. This innovative solution eliminates the need for refrigeration during the transportation and storage of vaccines, helping to improve access to vital immunizations globally.

news.headline: Czech central bank head advocates studying Bitcoin over fear, considering a test portfolio for reserves.
news.summary: The governor of the Czech National Bank has expressed interest in studying Bitcoin rather than viewing it with fear. The institution is contemplating the creation of a test portfolio consisting of Bitcoin for potential reserves.

news.headline: BWXT secures U.S. Naval contracts worth $2.1 billion for nuclear reactor components, to be manufactured at various locations.
news.summary: BWX Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: BWXT) recently secured contracts worth around $2.1 billion from the U.S. Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program. The contracts involve the production of reactor components for submarines like the Columbia and Virginia class, as well as aircraft carriers such as the Ford class. These manufacturing and material procurement tasks will be carried out at BWXT Nuclear Operations Group, Inc. facilities in Lynchburg, Virginia, in addition to locations in Barberton and Eu.

news.headline: Cantor Fitzgerald, led by Howard Lutnick, increased Bitcoin & Chinese tech exposure in Q4 2024. Added COIN, MSTR, MARA.更多详情请查看Benzinga.com
news.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald LP, run by US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, increased its exposure to Bitcoin and Chinese technology companies in Q4 of 2024. Lutnick's hedge fund made several changes during the quarter, including increasing its stake in Coinbase Global Inc. by 66% and adding new positions in Michael Saylor's Strategy Inc. (formerly MicroStrategy) and MARA Holdings Inc. These moves were made around the time of the US elections, with results declared on Nov. 5. Lutnick, who was Trump's pick for Commerce Secretary, saw these changes amid Trump's reelection. Full details available on Benzinga.com.

It forecasts a 15.7% annual growth, with the market expected to reach US$2 billion by 2025. The loyalty market in Italy has shown a consistent value increase, with a CAGR of 17.6% from 2020 to 2024. This report provides over 50 KPIs on loyalty program trends, segmented by end-use sectors, operational KPIs, retail product dynamics, and consumer demographics for the Q1 2025 Update.

news.headline: Extreme Networks introduces Platform ONE for MSPs, featuring AI-driven architecture and consumption-based billing for cost savings.
news.summary: Extreme Networks, Inc., a prominent player in AI-driven networking automation (Nasdaq: EXTR), revealed the imminent launch of Extreme Platform ONE™ tailored for Managed Service Providers (MSPs). This innovative platform operates on an exclusive AI-oriented multi-tenant structure, incorporating consumption-based billing and poolable licensing. Such a setup empowers MSPs with exceptional adaptability, ease of use, and financial efficiencies, facilitating seamless management of clients on a large scale. With Extreme's MSP program, providers are granted unparalleled capabilities.

news.headline: Keepit, a cloud backup vendor, sees exceptional growth in a challenging market, with record revenue boost in 2024.
news.summary: Denmark-based Keepit, a leading provider of cloud backup and recovery solutions for safeguarding SaaS data, has reported substantial growth and advancements, solidifying its place as a prominent player in the SaaS data protection sector. Throughout the year, the company has seen notable achievements such as record revenue increase, strategic expansions, and recognition from the industry. Keepit attributes its success to ongoing communication with customers, partners, and the community, highlighting its commitment to innovation and customer service.

news.headline: Hirschbach Motor Lines partners with Hyperscience for digital transformation, using AI to automate document processing.

As a successful and rapidly expanding business, Hirschbach opted for Hyperscience's enterprise AI to streamline document processing.

news.headline: The Juice acquires AudiencePlus to boost B2B marketing, merging teams and products. The Juice connects B2B marketers with over 100,000 professionals.
news.summary: An Indianapolis B2B content platform, The Juice, has confirmed its acquisition of audience marketing platform AudiencePlus, combining teams and products. Established by High Alpha in 2021 and supported by Ground Game Ventures, The Juice links B2B marketers with a network of 100,000+ B2B professionals to enhance content interaction. AudiencePlus, also a High Alpha portfolio company, was brought under The Juice's umbrella. The acquisition aims to strengthen their market presence and improve content delivery for both brands and consumers.

news.headline: Riley, an all-in-one parenting platform, raises $3.1M in seed funding for app supporting early parenthood.
news.summary: A technology startup in San Francisco, Riley, unveiled the closure of $3.1 million in seed funding and the introduction of a beta program for its parenting app. The app, designed to aid early parenthood, integrates AI technology and expert advice based on research findings to provide proactive and instantaneous assistance for parents. True Ventures spearheaded the funding round, with Flybridge and Next Wave NYC joining in as investors. Lindsay Kaplan, a prominent figure, also participated in the investment.

Anetac launches first User Group to promote cross-industry collaboration in identity security; includes members from finance, healthcare, retail sectors.
news.summary: Anetac, a top player in safeguarding businesses against identity-related risks in hybrid settings, unveiled its inaugural Anetac User Group in Los Altos, California. This development showcases a crucial step in the firm's dedication to enhancing identity security measures through cooperative innovation. The group convened for the first time on February 13, with participation from security experts in finance, healthcare, retail, and critical infrastructure industries. This event signifies a key stride in fostering industry collaboration and knowledge exchange among professionals focused on protecting identities.

news.headline: Altria reaffirms 2025 full-year earnings guidance at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference.
news.summary: Altria recently participated in the Consumer Analyst Group of New York conference, where they reaffirmed their full-year earnings guidance for 2025. The company, based in Richmond, Virginia, discussed their financial outlook and reiterated their commitment to achieving their earnings targets. This reaffirmation provides investors with assurance regarding Altria's projected financial performance in 2025. It signals the company's confidence in meeting its financial goals and maintaining stability in the coming years.

news.headline: Microwave transmission equipment market to reach US$ 8.75 billion by 2035, with 4.5% growth rate, driven by demand for high-speed data networks and 5G rollout, East Asia leading with 35.7% market share.
news.summary: A recent study by Fact.MR projects that the global microwave transmission equipment market will reach $5.65 billion in 2025 and is expected to reach $8.75 billion by 2035. The rise is driven by the push for energy-efficient systems adhering to global sustainability standards, with green microwave equipment gaining popularity. Microwave equipment is preferred over fiber-optic systems due to lower costs in maintenance and installation, particularly in areas with challenging terrain. The demand for these equipment is further boosted by the need for high-capacity data transmission networks driven by live streaming, video-on-demand, and high-definition TV services. East Asia leads the market with 35.7% global market share in 2025, with China and Japan's communication infrastructure projects being key factors. North America follows due to the need for high-speed connections and the emphasis on 5G and IoT applications. The market is forecasted to grow at a 4.5% CAGR from 2025 to 2035, with full-indoor equipment expected to see a 5.5% CAGR.

news.headline: Custodial workers at 2 Calgary school districts to strike, 800 CBE and 350 CCSD employees vote 94% in favor.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, the union representing custodial and maintenance workers at the Calgary Board of Education (CBE) and the Calgary Catholic School District (CCSD) is set to issue a 72-hour strike notice. Approximately 800 employees at the CBE, who are part of CUPE Local 40, voted with a 94.5% majority in favor of a strike last week. Additionally, 350 employees at CCSD, members of CUPE Local 520, also voted in favor of a strike with a 94% majority. CUPE Alberta President Rory Gill mentioned that the union plans to commence the strike on Monday morning.

news.headline: Report from Validated Insights shows OPM market shifting, with fee-for-service eclipsing revenue-share arrangements.
news.summary: In Chicago, a recent report from Validated Insights highlights a shift in the market for online program managers (OPM). The report indicates that fee-for-service arrangements are gaining momentum, surpassing revenue-share agreements. This data signifies a significant change in the industry landscape, with fee-for-service models becoming more prevalent. This trend is expected to have a notable impact on the market moving forward.

news.headline: VersaBank to report Q1 fiscal 2025 results on March 5, 2025, conference call/webcast at 9:00 a.m. ET. TSX: VBNK, NASDAQ: VBNK.
news.summary: VersaBank, a prominent financial institution (TSX: VBNK) (NASDAQ: VBNK), is set to reveal its first quarter fiscal 2025 results on March 5, 2025, at 7:00 a.m. ET. The bank, headquartered in London, ON, will not only disclose its financial performance for this period but also conduct a conference call to provide insights and discuss further details. This important event will offer stakeholders and investors a comprehensive overview of the bank's standing during the initial quarter of the fiscal year.

news.summary: Headquartered in Milpitas, California, SolarEdge Technologies, Inc. (Nasdaq: SEDG), a prominent player in smart energy solutions, revealed its financial performance for Q4 2024 and the full year concluding on December 31, 2024. news.headline: SysAid sees productivity gains with AI in ITSM; Recognized in 2025 Gartner Innovation Guide. Leading provider of Service Management solutions.

news.headline: New media agency Mile Marker forms from Cage Point & PlusMedia merger. Secures AOR assignments from Bridges Consumer Healthcare, Carlin Consumer Health, and Hiscox USA.

news.headline: Republic Business Credit hires Chris Costello as Senior Vice President, Underwriter, bringing decades of experience in Asset Based lending.
news.summary: Republic Business Credit has appointed Chris Costello as Senior Vice President of Underwriting, effective February 19, 2025. With years of experience in Asset Based lending, Costello will be a valuable addition to the underwriting team at Republic Business Credit in New Orleans. This move reflects the company's commitment to strengthening its underwriting capabilities.
